title: DockerManager
content:
# DockerManager


## Methods


### __init__() -> None


### login_to_docker_registry(registry_url: str, username: str, password: str) -> None


### build_kelvin_app_dockerfile(kelvin_app_building_object: KelvinAppBuildingObject) -> bool

Build the docker file used in the creation of the docker image.


### get_docker_images(labels: Optional[dict], image_ids: Optional[List[str]], image_parent_ids: Optional[List[str]], image_names: Optional[List[str]]) -> List[DockerImage]

Obtain a list of all docker images available in the system.


### build_kelvin_app_docker_image(kelvin_app_building_object: KelvinAppBuildingObject) -> bool

Build the docker image from the provided KelvinAppBuildingObject.


### build_docker_app_image(docker_build_object: DockerAppBuildingObject) -> bool

Build the docker image from the provided DockerAppBuildingObject.


### build_kelvin_v2_app_image(build_object: ProjectBuildingObject) -> bool

Build the docker image from the provided DockerAppBuildingObject.


### ensure_docker_image_exists(docker_image: str) -> str

Using the base logged client, ensure that the provided image is valid either in the currently logged registry or the in the public one (DockerHub).


### pull_docker_image_from_registry(docker_image_name: DockerImageName, auth_config: Optional[dict], tag_local_name: bool) -> str

Pull the specified docker image from the currently logged registry.


### remove_docker_image(docker_image_name: str) -> bool

Remove the specified docker image from the local system.


### prune_docker_images(filters: Optional[dict]) -> bool

A simple wrapper around the client to prune dangling docker images.


### check_if_docker_image_exists(docker_image_name: str, silent: bool, raise_exception: bool) -> bool

Check whether the specified docker image exists on the local system.


### extract_dir_from_docker_image(app_name: str, output_dir: str, container_dir: str) -> bool

Extract the content of the specified built application to the provided output directory.


### get_docker_containers(target_all_containers: bool, image_names: Optional[List[str]], container_names: Optional[List[str]], labels: Optional[dict]) -> List[DockerContainer]

Obtain a list of all docker containers available in the system.


### stop_containers(container_names: Optional[List[str]], image_names: Optional[List[str]]) -> bool

Internal function for stopping containers by either container name or image name.


### remove_container(container_name: str) -> bool

Remove the provided container from the system.


### prune_docker_containers(filters: Optional[dict]) -> bool

A simple wrapper around the client to prune dangling docker containers.
